description: This figure presents schematic cross-sectional illustrations of oxide nodules formed on Fe-Mn-Al alloys oxidized at 800°C, delineating three distinct structural types dependent on manganese concentration. Type 1 depicts large iron oxide nodules formed on alloys containing less than about 5% manganese, consisting of an outer Fe2O3 layer, an inner (Fe,Mn)3O4 region, and internal oxide particles of (Fe,Mn)Al2O4 or Al2O3. Type 2 shows large iron oxide nodules on alloys with approximately 5–7.5% manganese, featuring discontinuous Mn2O3 layers or particles at the gas-oxide interface, a Fe2O3 layer, a partial healing layer at the nodule base, and underlying (Fe,Mn)3O4 with (Fe,Mn)Al2O4 or Al2O3. Type 3 illustrates small manganese-rich nodules on alloys containing at least 7.5% manganese, characterized by a continuous outer Mn2O3 layer, an intermediate Fe2O3 layer, and a complete healing layer of (Fe,Mn)Al2O4 or Al2O3 at the metal-oxide interface. The progression demonstrates that increasing manganese content transforms large iron-rich nodules into smaller manganese-rich ones, promotes the formation of continuous Mn2O3 and aluminate layers, and enables complete healing layer formation, thereby retarding further oxidation by limiting ionic diffusion.
